title: Find the peak elements in an array

content.problemStatement:
Given an array `A` of size `N`, your task is to find and print all the peak elements in the array. A peak element is one that is strictly greater than its neighboring elements. For the first and last elements, only consider their single adjacent element.

If no peak element exists in the array, print `-1`.


Input Format

The first line contains the integer NNN — the size of array
The second line contains all the elements of array AAA


Output Format
Output all the peak elements in the array in the order they are present in the original array.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines: the first line contains an integer N — the size of the array. The second line contains N space‑separated integers A1, A2, …, AN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print all peak elements of the given array in the order they appear, separated by a single space. If a test case has no peak element, output -1. Print each test case's answer on its own line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Only the element 4 (at index 3) is strictly greater than both neighbours 2 and 3, so it is the sole peak.
7 > 3 (first element), 5 > 3 and > 2, 10 > 2 (last element). All three are peaks.
With a single element there are no neighbours, so the element is considered a peak.

solution.approach: A peak element is strictly greater than its immediate neighbours. By scanning the array once we can decide for each position i whether it satisfies the peak condition: (i == 0 || A[i] > A[i-1]) && (i == N-1 || A[i] > A[i+1]). Collect all such elements while preserving order. The scan is O(N) per test case and uses O(1) extra space besides the output buffer.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(total N) for the whole input.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) auxiliary space (output stored in a StringBuilder).

code.Java: import java.io.*;import java.util.*;public class Main{public static void main(String[] args) throws Exception{BufferedReader br=new BufferedReader(new InputStreamReader(System.in));StringBuilder out=new StringBuilder();int T=Integer.parseInt(br.readLine().trim());for(int tc=0;tc<T;tc++){int N=Integer.parseInt(br.readLine().trim());int[] A=new int[N];StringTokenizer st=new StringTokenizer(br.readLine());for(int i=0;i<N;i++)A[i]=Integer.parseInt(st.nextToken());boolean first=true;boolean any=false;for(int i=0;i<N;i++){boolean leftOk=(i==0)||A[i]>A[i-1];boolean rightOk=(i==N-1)||A[i]>A[i+1];if(leftOk&&rightOk){if(!first)out.append(' ');out.append(A[i]);first=false;any=true;}}if(!any)out.append("-1");if(tc!=T-1)out.append('\n');}System.out.print(out.toString());}}

def generate_case(i, RINT, POWER):

        t = 1
        print(t)
        n = 1
        print(n)
        print(1)

        t = 1
